title: Hanwha Semitech Nears Supply of Hybrid Bonder to SK Hynix - thelec.net
summary: Join us UPDATED. 2026-03-26 07:41 (목) Hanwha Semitech Nears Supply of Hybrid Bonder to SK Hynix Hanwha Semitech Nears Supply of Hybrid Bonder to SK Hynix JEONG IL JOO 승인 2026.03.26 07:39 댓글 0 이 기사를 공유합니다 Second-Generation System Developed With Prodrive Technologies Enters Qualification Phase Hanwha Semitech is expected to supply its second-generation hybrid bonding equipment to SK Hynix as early as next month, marking a potential step toward entering advanced semiconductor packaging production lines. The company recently brought its "SHB2 Nano" hybrid bonder into South Korea after assembling the system in the Netherlands and plans to begin qualification testing with SK Hynix, according to people familiar with the matter. [...] In addition, collaboration with Prodrive Technologies is likely to have increased development costs compared with in-house production, market observers said. Besi's hybrid bonders are priced at around 5 billion to 6 billion won per unit. Industry estimates suggest that domestically developed hybrid bonders could be priced at around 4 billion won, positioning them between TC bonders and Besi's systems. 저작권자 © THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media 무단전재 및 재배포 금지 JEONG IL JOO 다른기사 보기 댓글삭제 삭제한 댓글은 다시 복구할 수 없습니다. 그래도 삭제하시겠습니까? 댓글 0 댓글쓰기 계정을 선택하시면 로그인·계정인증을 통해 댓글을 남기실 수 있습니다. 회원 로그인 --- 최신순 추천순 --- [...] Hanwha Semitech generated more than 90 billion won in revenue last year from its TC bonder SFM5 Expert model, with SK Hynix as a key customer. The company previously supplied a first-generation hybrid bonder to SK Hynix in 2022 for research and development purposes. Samsung Electronics, meanwhile, is said to be evaluating a die-to-wafer (D2W) hybrid bonder developed by its affiliate Semes. The adoption of Hanwha Semitech's hybrid bonder in mass production will depend on its performance and cost competitiveness. The SHB2 Nano is believed to have been developed faster than Prodrive Technologies' typical timelines, raising concerns about potential completeness, according to people familiar with the project.

title: 2026 South Korea Private Capital Breakdown - PitchBook
summary: ### Global Private Debt Report The 2025 Annual Global Private Debt Report analyzes global private debt fundraising, fund performance, and cash flows. [...] Private equity deal value declined in 2025 even as the number of transactions increased slightly, highlighting the changing composition of deal activity within South Korea’s buyout market. South Korea Private Capital Breakdown Private Equity Venture Capital LinkedIn Twitter Facebook Email Additional Research ### US Evergreen Fund Landscape The Q1 2026 US Evergreen Fund Landscape report covers the rapid rise of evergreen funds, providing data and insights on assets, flows, returns, and more. ### PitchBook Analyst Note: When Access to VC Becomes a Liability Venture secondaries offer access to top private companies—but fraud, opacity, and weak oversight threaten to make them the next cautionary tale in private markets. ### Global Private Debt Report [...] Several structural forces continue to shape investment opportunities across South Korea’s private markets. Corporate carveouts driven by chaebol restructuring have become a notable source of private equity deal flow, while corporate venture capital accounted for more than one-third of venture transactions and over 60% of total deal value in 2025. Fundraising trends also highlight a widening divide between asset classes: Private equity raised $8.4 billion across just four funds in 2025—nearly 8x the capital raised by venture vehicles—illustrating the increasing institutionalization of South Korea’s buyout market even as the venture ecosystem undergoes a cyclical reset. title: Startups lead hiring at 8.9% Net Employment Change, BPO lowest at 0.1%: TeamLease report - Storyboard18
summary: Capability-led expansion across sectors Hiring in these sectors is increasingly focused on specialised roles. In startups, demand is concentrated in product engineering, supply chain optimisation and data-driven growth roles. Healthcare is expanding across clinical services, regulatory functions and digital health, while manufacturing is seeing demand for automation, semiconductor and advanced engineering roles. At the same time, the report noted that hiring is no longer volume-driven but aligned with capability depth and productivity. Moderation after high-growth phases Several sectors showed moderated growth after earlier expansion. Logistics, retail, FMCG and automotive all recorded positive but slower hiring momentum, reflecting calibrated workforce alignment. [...] Healthcare and pharmaceuticals followed with 7.0%, while manufacturing, engineering and infrastructure posted 6.6%, reflecting expansion driven by digital commerce, healthcare demand and industrial capex cycles. The report showed strong hiring intent across these sectors, with 80% of startups, 78% of healthcare firms, and 70% of manufacturing companies planning workforce expansion. title: SK hynix writes down Gauss Labs as Nvidia alliance drives AI manufacturing push - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosunbiz
summary: SK hynix adopted Panoptes for thin-film deposition just a month after its release. By Dec. 2024, it virtually measured about 50 million wafers and improved variation by about 29%. Variation is an indicator of the magnitude of quality fluctuation among produced products. The smaller the variation, the lower the likelihood of defects. Gauss Labs also released Panoptes 2.0, with improved performance, in Aug. 2024. SK hynix applied this product to the etching process in a bid to improve Production yield. [...] By Jeong Du-yong Published 2026.03.27. 06:01 The value of Gauss Labs, which SK hynix established with a full capital contribution, saying it would achieve manufacturing innovation through artificial intelligence (AI) technology, was shown to have declined sharply. Gauss Labs launched in Aug. 2020, a year after Chey Tae-won, SK Group chair, said, "If we fail to leverage innovative technology, we cannot provide collateral for SK's future," and called for adopting AI. It began operations under the banner of "the first AI specialist corporations in SK Group," but is seen as having produced no notable business results more than five years after its founding. [...] It is also notable that the book value SK hynix adjusted this time is about 2,800,000,000 won lower than Gauss Labs' total assets (28,141,000,000 won). An industry official said, "SK hynix effectively assessed that Gauss Labs has limited potential for business expansion." ◇ SK hynix process improvement with virtual metrology solutions

title: Stryker says manufacturing mostly restored after cyberattack - iTnews

Stryker's staff found that mobile phones, laptops and other remote devices ​that were running Microsoft's ⁠Windows operating system and ⁠could connect to its computer platforms had been impacted ​by the attack. Stands Up to Iran #### The Social-Media Shakedown Begins [...] 0700 GMT – China Telecom earnings face a bleak outlook, says Morningstar’s Dan Baker in a note. The company’s increased expenses dragged its 2025 Ebitda and net profit lower despite a flattish revenue, he notes. Industrial digitization, which used to be the Chinese telecommunications operator’s growth engine, showed a small top line decline, which appears worrying, he adds. A 3.0% value-added tax increase is poised to come into effect soon. This is likely to weigh on China Telecom’s earnings, the analyst notes, adding that he expects the company’s net profit to fall 9.0% in 2026 and cuts his near-term earnings projections by 15%-20%. Morningstar lowers its fair-value estimate by 15% to HK$5.80. Shares fall 0.4% to HK$4.89. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

title: South Korea's Overseas Investment Rebounds 8.7% After Two-Year Decline - 조선일보
summary: 2 Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix Plunge Amid TurboQuant, Iran War Fears 3 Google's TurboQuant Sparks Semiconductor Stock Plunge 4 Song Ji-hyo Halts YouTube Amid 'Running Man' Controversy 5 Tonight Show Host's North Korea Joke Angers BTS Fans 6 South Korean Universities Decline in Computer Science, AI Rankings 7 Fire at Yeongdeok Wind Power Complex Kills Three Maintenance Workers 8 Cuba's Blackouts Force Residents to Use Phone Lights 9 South Korea Introduces Separate Taxation for High-Dividend Stocks 10 Ukraine Deploys Humanoid Robots in Battlefield Reconnaissance 6 South Korean Universities Decline in Computer Science, AI Rankings 7 Fire at Yeongdeok Wind Power Complex Kills Three Maintenance Workers 8 Cuba's Blackouts Force Residents to Use Phone Lights 9 [...] By region, investment in Asia increased by 23.9% to 16.06 billion USD, the largest growth rate. Asia's share of total investment also rose by 4.3 percentage points, from 18.0% to 22.3%. This growth was driven by surges in manufacturing investment in China (33%), Indonesia (58%), and Vietnam (27%). · This article has been translated by Upstage Solar AI. 원문보기 (View Original Korean Article) Editor’s Pick []( Trump Extends Suspension of Iranian Energy Attacks to April 6 5h ago |Park Kook-hee []( Drug Deliveries Outpace Takeout, Targeting South Korean Youth 10h ago |Kim Hee-rae,Kim Na-yeong []( Han Kang's 'We Do Not Part' Wins NBCC Fiction Award 1h ago |Choi Hye-seung Most Read News 1 McDonald's Launches KPop Demon Hunters Meals Featuring HUNTR/X, Saja Boys 2 [...] A sign reading 'Samsung Expressway' stands in front of Samsung Electronics' semiconductor plant site in Taylor, U.S. Authorities decided to name the new road passing the site after the company in late 2022 to commemorate Samsung's large-scale investment. The road, which crosses vast barley and corn fields, bears the name of a Korean company. /Courtesy of Aurora correspondent By industry, the financial and insurance sector surged by 32.7% to 37.89 billion USD, driving the overall rebound. It accounted for more than half (53%) of total investment. In contrast, the manufacturing sector grew by only 4.1% to 17.11 billion USD.

title: District 218 plans to renovate main entrances of Eisenhower and Richards high schools - Chicago Tribune
summary: elewis@chicagotribune.com ### Share this: Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Share on Bluesky (Opens in new window) Bluesky Share on X (Opens in new window) X Print (Opens in new window) Print Email a link to a friend (Opens in new window) Email ## More in Daily Southtown ### Moraine Valley teacher, flautist, retiring after nearly 3 decades of ‘making music tick’ ### Will County Board Republicans call for Jacqueline Traynere’s resignation ### Donna Vickroy: The heartbreaking, heart-lifting search for Dan Davis ### High School District 230 board questions 12-year extension of Palos Heights TIF district 2026 March 20 [...] ### Share this: Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Share on Bluesky (Opens in new window) Bluesky Share on X (Opens in new window) X Print (Opens in new window) Print Email a link to a friend (Opens in new window) Email By Evy Lewis | elewis@chicagotribune.com | Daily Southtown PUBLISHED: Getting your Trinity Audio player ready... Plans to renovate the main entrances of Dwight D. Eisenhower High School, in Blue Island, and Harold L. Richards High School, in Oak Lawn, are moving forward. The project will cost $4.26 million and will be constructed using existing fund balances, meaning no new debt will be taken out for the construction, according to High School District 218 public information specialist Andrea Azzo. [...] The project’s goal is to “establish a modern, well-defined main entrance” at the two high schools, Azzo said. “It’ll make a big difference,” said Superintendent Josh Barron. “Much more welcoming and customer service-oriented, and more functional.” The District 218 board unanimously approved during a meeting March 18. The layout of Eisenhower High School’s main entrance will be updated to connect the administrative and counseling offices to the main entrance, while the facade at Richards High School will be updated to resolve water issues, Azzo said. Design options are still under discussion. Pepper Construction and Ujamaa Construction will work on the project, Azzo said. The district has worked with the two construction firms on other projects. elewis@chicagotribune.com

title: Agriculture under pressure as "greenhouses become strategic assets" - Hortidaily
summary: The international geopolitical context always has an impact on the greenhouse sector. "Right now, the biggest effect involves the price of steel, which has risen by around 10% since the start of the war in Iran, after years of stability. Clearly, rising steel and raw material prices are reflected in the cost of greenhouses. Luckily the impact is still minimal, and we have not yet noticed a drop in demand for our solutions. We are however worried about the war continuing, for humanitarian reasons as well as because of the effect it will have on our industry." © CO.SER [...] © CO.SER It is impossible not to think back to the Covid-19 period and not make a comparison. "Unfortunately, the current situation reminds us of the pandemic. Even then, the impact was minimal at first, but we saw the effects the following year, with steel rising by more than 100% and doubled greenhouse prices. This led to a slow demand, due both to high prices and the uncertainty of the period." According to Cipro, the sector had found a new balance in recent years: prices stabilised - albeit not to pre-Covid levels - and demand for greenhouses was lively again. "At the beginning of 2026, however, we find ourselves once again in a situation of uncertainty, with dynamics that are still evolving and require careful investment planning." © CO.SER [...] ## Related Articles → See More ### Agriculture under pressure as "greenhouses become strategic assets" ### Customized-solutions empower high-efficiency sweet pepper farming ### Retrofit or rebuild?
